name: Network Attack & Defense
description: Network-focused lab covering reconnaissance, firewall analysis, man-in-the-middle attacks, and network hardening. Students learn both offensive network techniques and defensive measures to secure enterprise infrastructure.

objectives.title: Network Reconnaissance
objectives.description: Perform comprehensive network reconnaissance to map the network topology, discover live hosts, and identify the operating systems and services running on each target.
hints: Use nmap with -sn for host discovery, then follow up with detailed service scans.
Try traceroute to understand the network path and identify routing devices.

objectives.title: Firewall Rule Analysis
objectives.description: Analyze the pfsense firewall configuration to identify overly permissive rules, misconfigurations, and potential bypass opportunities.
hints: Use nmap to probe firewall rules from different network segments.
Look for rules that allow unnecessary inbound traffic or use 'any' too broadly.

objectives.title: MITM Attack
objectives.description: Execute a man-in-the-middle attack using ARP spoofing to intercept network traffic between two target hosts and capture credentials.
hints: Use arpspoof or ettercap to perform ARP poisoning between targets.
Capture traffic with Wireshark and look for plaintext credentials in HTTP or FTP.

objectives.title: Port Scanning & Service Identification
objectives.description: Perform detailed port scanning to identify all running services, their versions, and potential vulnerabilities on the vulnerable router and servers.
hints: Use nmap -sV for service version detection and -sC for default scripts.
Check identified service versions against CVE databases for known vulnerabilities.

objectives.title: Firewall Hardening
objectives.description: Harden the pfsense firewall by implementing proper rules to block the attacks performed earlier while maintaining legitimate network functionality.
hints: Implement egress filtering and restrict outbound traffic to necessary ports only.
Enable ARP inspection or static ARP entries to prevent ARP spoofing.

objectives.title: Network Defense Report
objectives.description: Write a comprehensive network defense report documenting all vulnerabilities found, attacks executed, and hardening measures implemented with before/after comparisons.
hints: Include network diagrams showing the topology and attack paths.
Document each firewall rule change with justification.
